categories.name: Story
categories.description: Fictional and narrative books including novels and classics.

categories.name: Tech
categories.description: Programming, software engineering, and computer science related books.

categories.name: Science
categories.description: Books related to physics, biology, astronomy, and general scientific knowledge.

categories.name: History
categories.description: Books covering world history, civilizations, wars, and historical events.

categories.name: Self-Help
categories.description: Books focused on personal growth, productivity, mindset, and well-being.

categories.name: Philosophy
categories.description: Books exploring ethics, existence, knowledge, and the nature of reality.

books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.description: Set in the Jazz Age on Long Island, this novel depicts narrator Nick Carraway's interactions with mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and Gatsby's obsession with Daisy Buchanan. A timeless story of wealth, class, love, and the American Dream.

books.title: Clean Code
books.author: Robert C. Martin
books.description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship. Teaches how to write clean, maintainable, and efficient code. Essential reading for every professional developer.

books.title: A Brief History of Time
books.author: Stephen Hawking
books.description: Explores the universe from the Big Bang to black holes, explaining time, space, and cosmology in an accessible and engaging way.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
books.author: Harper Lee
books.description: A story of racial injustice and moral growth in the American South seen through young Scout Finch and her father, lawyer Atticus Finch.

books.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
books.author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
books.description: Practical advice for improving software development skills and productivity. Covers best practices, pitfalls, and the mindset of a master programmer.

books.title: Cosmos
books.author: Carl Sagan
books.description: A journey through the universe, exploring science, space, and humanity's place among the stars. One of the most celebrated science books ever written.

books.author: George Orwell
books.description: A dystopian novel about surveillance, totalitarianism, and loss of individuality in a society ruled by Big Brother. One of the most important books of the 20th century.

books.title: You Don't Know JS
books.author: Kyle Simpson
books.description: Deep dive into JavaScript concepts like scope, closures, and prototypes. Teaches the language from the ground up for developers who want true mastery.

books.title: The Selfish Gene
books.author: Richard Dawkins
books.description: Explains evolution from the perspective of genes as the unit of natural selection. A landmark work that reshaped how we understand life on Earth.

books.title: The Alchemist
books.author: Paulo Coelho
books.description: A mystical journey of a shepherd boy searching for his destiny and treasure. An inspiring fable about following your dreams and listening to your heart.

books.title: Introduction to Algorithms
books.author: Thomas H. Cormen
books.description: Comprehensive textbook on algorithms, data structures, and computational theory. Used in universities worldwide and essential for every serious programmer.

books.title: The Origin of Species
books.author: Charles Darwin
books.description: Foundational work on evolution by natural selection that forever changed our understanding of life. Darwin's meticulous observations shaped modern biology.

books.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: A sweeping history of the human race from ancient foragers to modern civilization. Harari examines how biology and history have defined us and enhanced our understanding of what it means to be human.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.description: A proven framework for building good habits and breaking bad ones. Clear reveals how tiny changes in behavior can lead to remarkable results over time.

books.title: Meditations
books.author: Marcus Aurelius
books.description: Personal writings of the Roman Emperor Marcus Aurelius reflecting Stoic philosophy. A timeless guide to virtue, resilience, and living a meaningful life.

books.title: The Guns of August
books.author: Barbara Tuchman
books.description: A gripping account of the opening weeks of World War I. Tuchman weaves together military strategy and political miscalculation in one of history's most devastating conflicts.

books.title: Deep Work
books.author: Cal Newport
books.description: A compelling argument for cultivating focused, distraction-free work in an increasingly noisy world. Newport provides practical strategies to develop the ability to concentrate intensely.

books.title: The Republic
books.author: Plato
books.description: One of the most influential works in Western philosophy. Plato explores justice, the ideal state, and the nature of the philosopher-king through Socratic dialogue.

books.title: Homo Deus
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: A bold vision of humanity's future exploring how technology might help us overcome death, unhappiness, and scarcity — and what new challenges will arise along the way.

books.title: The Power of Now
books.author: Eckhart Tolle
books.description: A guide to spiritual enlightenment and living fully in the present moment. Tolle draws on personal experience and wisdom traditions to show the path to inner peace.

books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.description: A psychological thriller following Raskolnikov, a destitute student who commits a murder and struggles with guilt and redemption in 19th-century St. Petersburg.

books.title: Design Patterns
books.author: Gang of Four
books.description: The classic catalog of 23 software design patterns. An essential reference for object-oriented software engineers describing reusable solutions to common design problems.

books.title: The Story of Civilization
books.author: Will Durant
books.description: A monumental history of human civilization covering art, politics, religion, science, and philosophy from ancient times to the modern era.

books.title: Beyond Good and Evil
books.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
books.description: Nietzsche challenges the foundations of traditional morality and presents his vision of a new philosophical order. A provocative and influential work in modern philosophy.
